data: The United Nations (UN) is an international organization established after World War II to promote peace, security, cooperation and friendly relations among nations. It is the largest intergovernmental organization in the world.

rows: Arabic, Chinese, English, French, Russian, Spanish

data:
Main Objectives:
• Maintain international peace and security
• Develop friendly relations among nations
• Promote human rights
• Support sustainable development
• Provide humanitarian assistance

Important UN Agencies:
• WHO
• UNESCO
• UNICEF
• FAO
• ILO
• IMF
• World Bank
• UNDP
• UNEP
• WFP

Exam Points:
• Founded after World War II.
• UN Day: 24 October.
• Security Council has 15 members.
• Permanent Members (P5): USA, UK, France, Russia, China.
• Headquarters: New York.
